name: Jalisco (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: loyalty, justice and the historic colours of Guadalajara
wealth and the historic colours of Guadalajara

description: Two equal vertical stripes of blue (hoist) and yellow (fly), with the coat of arms of Jalisco centred over both.
history: Adopted on 7 May 2011 by the Congress of the State of Jalisco, formalising a flag that had been used unofficially since the late 20th century. The colours derive from the 1539 grant of arms to the city of Guadalajara by Emperor Charles V.

features.symbolism: Blue and yellow are the historic colours of the city of Guadalajara and of the old Kingdom of Nueva Galicia, of which Jalisco is the heir.

features.role: coat of arms of Jalisco (two gold lions rampant on blue, with pine trees, surmounted by a banner)
features.symbolism: The arms derive from those granted by Charles V to the city of Guadalajara on 8 November 1539: two gold lions rampant standing against a pine tree on a blue field, recalling Guadalajara in Castile (the namesake), with the helmet and banner of Spanish heraldic tradition.
